reviews: the lecture is basically just time to do the worksheets that are due weekly (graded on completion and effort not accuracy) and he guides you through them step by step. the quizzes are based on what was gone over in class and he usually formats it the same way as the worksheets. he is playfully sarcastic and easily sidetracked (less work/easy ec)
He is very sarcastic. He doesn't apologize when he says sorry. He makes us know things.
hes terrible and not understanding of difficult situations or circumstances

He is one of the best professors at Temple. His teaching style is very good and easy to follow. His exams are SO fair and he will not test you on something he hasn't taught in class. No homework and LOTs of extra credit opportunities. Quizzes (~biweekly) make up most of the grade. Highly recommend for Tech Chem.
I struggled through the class but he gave good explanations for stuff. Sometimes things were a bit unclear but he was really good with communicating important updates.

He is just not a good professor. He doesn't explain anything during lectures and just does a bunch of random problems on the board. If you did not take AP chem in high school, then this class is going to be very difficult. Before every exam, I had to teach myself all of the material because he doesn't teach and just assumes you know everything.

TAKEN ONLINE. used one note for lecture ,overall not much different from in person class. does not waste anyones time, material taught is straight forward and can be found in the textbook for more info. Quizzes were difficult. Overall not good teacher

Focus on in class examples over assigned homework for test and you should do fine. All notes are posted online and LOTS of extra credit chances. I found the quizzes very difficult more so than the exams. Quizzes are out of 25 points so not a lot of room for error on problems.

Took Keyer for Tech 1. Really a cool guy, you can ask him any question whenver and he will do his best to give you the best answer. Gives plenty of extra credit and is willing to talk with you if you're having problems. Highly recommend.

He posts all his lectures, and questions on exams are almost the same as what he does in class. He flat out states that he does not hold your hand and expects seniors to try. It's not easy but if you meet with him, he is helpful.

I dont like Keyer, period. He never seemed willing to help and obviously chooses favorites. Course is hard as expected, no complaints there. Lots of papers and work to do, but Keyer makes the class unpleasant.

all right teacher, teaches all necessary content and the course is fairly straightforward. Its demanding as expected for a 5 credit course. The work was fine and manageable. Biggest complaint was him. His attitude towards some students is demeaning. Gave assignments over breaks. Just in general not a good person. Made me dislike course.

I really enjoyed my semester with him. He is nice and willing to help if you ask for it. If you take him for tech 2, the first lab reports you hand in might not be so great but there are rewrites and you'll definitely get a better grade if you edit your work. If you  put in the effort you won't fail his class.

Keyers awesome. End of story. he helps his students thoroughly understand the subject. He's great with office hours and he's willing to help his students. He's understanding and will give extra credit assignments when he sees the class struggling. He's definitely awesome and I recommend him to everyone :)
blah blah
He's not bad, but you have to keep up with the reading. And you have to be good at math.

Hes a nice guy. If you actually do the homework (unlike me), or bother studying for the quizzes (unlike me), you will be able to easily get an A (unlike me). Even if you dont know how to do the problems, just write something down and he'll give you some credit. A lot of the stuff in class is review so you'll have a tendency to blow it off, but dont

The class is so easy. I barely opened the book ever. The tests come right from the notes and problems in class. He uses exact problems done in class with just different elements. Just read the notes and take your time in lab don't rush and your fine... Goto every class and your fine. I studied the night before the exams and got nearly 100s on them.

the class is not so bad... all you need to do is do good in lab, quizzes and tests. lol, seriously though, you need to keep up. he does curve. he seems very friendly when he speaks to the class in general, but it seems like he feels bothered when you approach him for a one on one. Who knows???????
He bites. Take Dr. Thomas, he actually likes to teach and cares about his students. Just take Thomas first and save yourself the agony.

Not a bad guy.  He really isn't as difficult as some people make it seem.  Yeah, you have to learn some stuff on your own but it is a big lecture...what do you expect?  Don't let him scare you.  He isn't so bad:)
Yeah he sucks..really hard tests..just took his final an hour ago and it was impossible! Don't take him, he doesn't care if you fail.
No Comments
he is the awesome teacher i have ever had, students who complin are lazy, he is great and teaches well
Study his old exams and you can do well.

His exam is extremely hard. He makes very creative problems each time; I studied his previous exam, but that didn't help at all.  I took Thomas' class last semester and thought he was the worst, but no, KEYER IS EVEN WORSE. They don't have any good professor in chemistry department...

Ugh, he's horrible. His slide shows are boring and not informative at all. His tests are extremely hard and his lectures will put anyone to sleep. I sugest not taking the class with him! He hardly explains anything and really doesnt care whether you pass or fail.

Avoid this guy. Like the plague. He's horribly boring, does nothing to help his students, and just basically sucks as a teacher. People ask him questions and he *tries* to answer. Don't take his class. Please. For the both of us.

I dont know y every1 likes him!he takes attendance and is mean! he doesnt like to help u, doesnt tolerate lateness,doesnt like to give retest,n wont spare one min for u to finish bubbling in the tests and he doesnt curve much either he sucks
good teacher

Awesome teacher! Gives powerpoint presentations you can get from his site. He's very good at what he does and enjoys chemistry! I wish I had him for Chem 1 [I have him for Chem 2]. I would have got a better grade!
Not that hard of a teacher, explains most of the material well. Exams and long and not as difficult as other genchem teachers

he seems hard prof but he is not, just concentrate his HW and quizez, you will definately end up with an A. If you don't understand his lectures, just don't worry just go over couple times after the class by urself basically self explaintory. But crucial is quizez and HW ( keep in mind).
he is the only one who teach that class. extremely long exams. Do the homework, quizzess 10 times, he will ask from that. lab is easy.
He's definitely the best general chem professor.  He is an awesome teacher and really breaks down Chemistry for you.  I would definitely recommend him to anyone taking GenChem.
Knows a lot about the subject matter, but doesn't offer any information that isn't in the book as other Chem teachers have.  Test are a little long, but not too difficult.

he is the best general chem professor. he actually teaches what is in the book, also you can get previous midterm & finals (which are exactly like the real thing) from him or the chem club. definitely try and get him over the other guys, you wont regret i
